title: SICP — Full Course Assignments (Python / Scheme / SQL)
summary: Complete SICP course covering functional programming, data structures, OOP, Scheme, SQL, and interpreter construction across 10 homework assignments, 8 lab exercises, and 4 projects (Hog, Cats, Ants, Scheme Interpreter). Assignments progress from Python basics through higher-order functions, recursion, data abstraction, OOP, Scheme, and SQL, culminating in a full Scheme interpreter as the capstone project.
instructionPreview: This task covers a full SICP-style programming-languages sequence: 10 homework assignments, 8 labs, and 4 projects spanning functional programming in Python, Scheme, and SQL. You must implement all stubbed functions across all assignments so that all tests pass.
instruction:
This task covers a full SICP-style programming-languages sequence: 10 homework assignments, 8 labs, and 4 projects spanning functional programming in Python, Scheme, and SQL. You must implement all stubbed functions across all assignments so that all tests pass.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The assignments are organized as follows:

## Modules to Implement

**Homework Assignments (hw01–hw10)**:

- **hw02** (`hw02/hw02.py`): Higher-order functions — implement `product`, `accumulate`, `summation_using_accumulate`, `product_using_accumulate`, `get_the_cake`, and `protected_secret`. Functions marked with `"*** YOUR CODE HERE ***"`. (~6 functions)
- **hw03** (`hw03/hw03.py`): Recursion — implement functions for digit manipulation, number partitioning, and sequence operations. (~5 functions)
- **hw04** (`hw04/hw04.py`, `hw04/shakespeare.py`, `hw04/ADT.py`): Data abstraction and tree recursion — implement tree construction helpers, Shakespeare text generation with successor tables, and the `is_tree` validator. (~12 functions)
- **hw05** (`hw05/hw05.py`): Object-oriented programming — implement class methods for a mutable linked-list and tree data structures. (~5 functions)
- **hw06** (`hw06/hw06.py`): OOP with inheritance — implement a `VendingMachine` class and `Cat`/`NoisyCat` classes with health and vocalization mechanics. (~4 functions)
- **hw07** (`hw07/hw07.py`): Linked lists and trees — implement list mutation operations, tree path generation, coin counting with trees, BST validation, cycle detection, and tree balancing. (~5 functions)
- **hw08** (`hw08/hw08.scm`): Scheme programming — implement Scheme functions for list processing, filtering, accumulation, and list manipulation. (~8 functions)
- **hw09** (`hw09/hw09.scm`): Scheme macros and streams — implement tail-recursive helpers, macro transformations, and stream operations. (~8 functions)
- **hw10** (`hw10/hw10.sql`): SQL queries — write queries involving joins, aggregation, grouping, and subqueries on a database of student records. (~4 queries)

**Lab Assignments (lab01–lab11)**:

- **lab01** (`lab01/lab01.py`): Python basics — implement `factorial`, triangle validation, and digit extraction. (~3 functions)
- **lab02** (`lab02/lab02.py`): Higher-order functions and currying — implement `count_cond`, `composite_identity`, and `cycle`. (~3 functions)
- **lab03** (`lab03/lab03.py`): Recursion and tree recursion — implement recursive functions for digit counting, interleaving, and number splitting. (~3 functions)
- **lab04** (`lab04/lab04.py`): Tree and mobile data abstractions — implement weight calculation, balance checking, tree summation, preorder traversal, path finding, and list insertion. (~8 functions)
- **lab05** (`lab05/lab05.py`): Mutable sequences and iterators — implement list mutation and iterator-based operations. (~1 function)
- **lab07** (`lab07/lab07.py`): OOP, linked lists, and trees — implement complex number representations, linked-list mutations, and tree pruning. (~4 functions)
- **lab09** (`lab09/expr.py`, `lab09/reader.py`, `lab09/buffer.py`): Calculator language interpreter — implement expression evaluation, tokenization, parsing, and input buffering for a prefix-notation calculator. (~10 functions)
- **lab10** (`lab10/lab10.scm`): Scheme — implement list operations, tree procedures, and higher-order Scheme functions. (~10 functions)
- **lab11** (`lab11/lab11.sql`): SQL — write SQL queries with filtering, aggregation, and conditional expressions. (~6 queries)

**Projects**:

- **pro01 — Hog** (`pro01/hog.py`): A dice game simulator — implement dice rolling with Sow Sad rules, Picky Piggy scoring, turn logic, Swine Swap detection, the full game loop, and player strategies. (~12 functions)
- **pro02 — Cats** (`pro02/cats.py`): Typing speed test — implement topic-based paragraph selection, word accuracy measurement, WPM calculation, autocorrect with edit distance, and a real-time typing report system. (~10 functions)
- **pro03 — Ants** (`pro03/ants.py`): A tower-defense game (Ants vs. Bees) — implement the full game engine including place connectivity, insect health/damage, ant deployment with food costs, container ants, special ant abilities (harvesting, throwing, fire, hunger, body-guarding, tanking, slowing, scaring, laser), bee movement and stinging, the hive invasion strategy, and the game simulation loop. (~40+ functions across 15+ classes)
- **pro04 — Scheme Interpreter** (`pro04/`): A Scheme language interpreter — implement the core evaluation/application cycle, environment frames with lexical scoping, special form handlers (define, if, cond, and, or, let, lambda, mu, quasiquote), built-in procedures (arithmetic, list operations, equality, filtering, reduction), the tokenizer and reader, and Scheme utility functions. (~30+ functions across 9 files)

## Stub Patterns

- Python files use `"*** YOUR CODE HERE ***"` or `pass  # TODO: implement` as stubs
- Scheme files use `'YOUR-CODE-HERE` or `'replace-this-line` as stubs
- SQL files use `'YOUR CODE HERE'` as stubs
- Code regions between `# BEGIN PROBLEM N` / `# END PROBLEM N` markers indicate problem boundaries

moduleDag.project: SICP — Full Course Assignments (Python / Scheme / SQL)
moduleDag.description: Complete SICP course covering functional programming, data structures, OOP, Scheme, SQL, and interpreter construction across 10 homework assignments, 8 lab exercises, and 4 projects (Hog, Cats, Ants, Scheme Interpreter). Assignments progress from Python basics through higher-order functions, recursion, data abstraction, OOP, Scheme, and SQL, culminating in a full Scheme interpreter as the capstone project.

nodes.label: Lab 01 — Python Basics
nodes.description: Foundational Python exercises: implement factorial, triangle-side validation, and digit extraction. Entry point of the curriculum before any HOF or recursion.

nodes.label: HW 02 — Higher-Order Functions
nodes.description: Implement product, accumulate, summation_using_accumulate, product_using_accumulate, get_the_cake, and protected_secret using iteration and function composition.

nodes.label: Lab 02 — HOF and Currying
nodes.description: Implement count_cond, composite_identity, and cycle — reinforcing HOF concepts through currying, function composition, and predicate-driven iteration.

nodes.label: HW 03 — Recursion
nodes.description: Implement recursive functions for digit manipulation (number_of_six, pingpong), counting partitions, and sequence operations. Introduces structural and tree recursion as the primary programming paradigm.

nodes.label: HW 10 — SQL Queries
nodes.description: Write SQL queries involving joins, aggregation, grouping, and subqueries on a student-records database. Independent SQL track pursued in parallel with Python/Scheme.

nodes.label: Lab 03 — Recursion and Tree Recursion
nodes.description: Implement recursive functions for digit counting, sequence interleaving, and number splitting — deepening tree recursion before data abstraction is introduced.

nodes.label: Project 01 — Hog
nodes.description: Implement a dice game simulator: Sow Sad rolling rules, Picky Piggy extra-turn scoring, Swine Swap detection, the full game loop, and higher-order player strategies (always_roll, make_averaged, bacon_strategy, etc.).

nodes.label: Project 02 — Cats
nodes.description: Implement a typing speed test: paragraph selection, per-word accuracy measurement, WPM calculation, autocorrect using recursive edit-distance, and a real-time typing report system.

nodes.label: HW 08 — Scheme Programming
nodes.description: First Scheme assignment: implement Scheme functions for list processing, filtering, and accumulation. Entry point for the Scheme sub-track, introduced once Python recursion is solid.

nodes.label: Lab 11 — SQL Advanced Queries
nodes.description: Write SQL queries with filtering, aggregation, and conditional expressions — extending hw10 SQL skills to more complex query patterns.

nodes.label: HW 04 — Data Abstraction and Trees
nodes.description: Implement Shakespeare text generation with successor tables (shakespeare.py), city/geography data abstraction (ADT.py), and tree construction/traversal helpers (hw04.py). Introduces data abstraction as a design principle applied to trees.

nodes.label: Lab 10 — Scheme List and Tree Operations
nodes.description: Implement Scheme list operations, tree procedures, and higher-order Scheme functions — building Scheme fluency with recursive list/tree patterns after hw08 introduces the language.

nodes.label: HW 09 — Scheme Macros and Streams
nodes.description: Implement tail-recursive helpers, macro transformations, and lazy stream operations in Scheme — extending Scheme knowledge to advanced language features.

nodes.label: Lab 04 — Tree and Mobile Data Abstractions
nodes.description: Implement weight calculation, balance checking, tree summation, preorder traversal, path finding, and list insertion using the tree ADT. Applies the data abstraction concepts from hw04.

nodes.label: HW 05 — OOP: Mutable Data Structures
nodes.description: Implement class methods for a mutable Link and Tree class — first OOP assignment, introducing mutation, __repr__, and object identity on familiar data structures.

nodes.label: Lab 05 — Mutable Sequences and Iterators
nodes.description: Implement list mutation operations and iterator-based sequence processing — reinforcing mutable state and Python iterator protocol after hw05 introduces OOP.

nodes.label: HW 06 — OOP with Inheritance
nodes.description: Implement a VendingMachine class and Cat/NoisyCat subclasses with health and vocalization mechanics — introduces class hierarchies, super(), and polymorphism through inheritance.

nodes.label: Lab 07 — OOP, Linked Lists, and Tree Pruning
nodes.description: Implement complex number representations via OOP, linked-list mutation operations, and tree pruning — consolidates the full OOP track before the large Ants project.

nodes.label: HW 07 — Advanced Linked Lists and Trees
nodes.description: Implement list mutation, tree path generation, coin counting, BST validation, cycle detection, and tree balancing — advanced algorithmic work on the linked-list and tree classes from the OOP track.

nodes.label: Project 03 — Ants
nodes.description: Implement a full tower-defense game engine: Place connectivity, insect health and damage, ant deployment with food costs, container ants, 15+ specialised ant classes (harvester, thrower, fire, bodyguard, tank, slow, scary, laser), bee movement, hive invasion strategy, and the simulation loop.

nodes.label: Lab 09 — Calculator Language Interpreter
nodes.description: Implement a prefix-notation calculator interpreter: Buffer (tokenisation with lookahead), Expr/CallExpr/LambdaExpr AST classes, and a recursive-descent reader/parser. Internal chain: buffer.py → expr.py → reader.py.

nodes.label: Project 04 — Scheme Interpreter
nodes.description: Implement a complete Scheme interpreter across 9 files: Frame/LambdaProcedure/ MuProcedure environment classes (scheme_classes.py), the eval/apply cycle (scheme_eval_apply.py), special form handlers for define/if/cond/let/lambda/ quasiquote (scheme_forms.py), built-in arithmetic and list procedures (scheme_builtins.py), tokeniser (scheme_tokens.py), and Scheme utility exercises (questions.scm).

edges.label: Python basics (control flow, functions) must be solid before higher-order functions
Python basics required before HOF currying exercises in lab02
HOF concepts (accumulate, product) are building blocks for recursive thinking in hw03
Currying and function composition from lab02 inform the recursive patterns in hw03
Hog player strategies are higher-order functions (make_averaged, bacon_strategy) requiring hw02 patterns
Hog's recursive scoring rules and strategy combinators require recursion from hw03
Cats autocorrect uses recursive edit-distance; paragraph selection uses recursive filtering
hw03 introduces recursion; lab03 deepens it with tree recursion practice
Tree construction and traversal in hw04 require tree recursion from hw03
Tree recursion patterns from lab03 are directly applied to tree data abstraction in hw04
Scheme is introduced after recursion in Python is solid; hw08 immediately uses recursive list patterns
lab11 extends hw10 SQL skills to more complex joins, aggregations, and conditionals
lab04 exercises use the tree ADT (tree/label/branches/is_leaf) introduced conceptually in hw04
lab10 requires Scheme syntax and list-recursion fluency established in hw08
hw09 macros and streams build directly on the Scheme foundations from hw08
hw05 introduces mutable OOP versions of the same tree/linked structures worked with in lab04
lab05 mutable sequences and iterators extend the mutable data structure concepts from hw05
hw06 OOP inheritance builds on the mutation and object-state concepts practised in lab05
lab07 requires class hierarchies and linked-list mutations introduced via hw06 inheritance
hw07 advanced list/tree algorithms operate on the linked-list and tree classes consolidated in lab07
Ants requires fluency with the full OOP stack (inheritance, mutation, linked structures) from lab07
lab09 calculator interpreter uses recursive tree evaluation and linked-list-like buffer structures from hw07
pro04 Scheme interpreter applies the same read-parse-eval pattern built in lab09
pro04 must handle Scheme macros and tail calls introduced in hw09
pro04 Scheme built-ins operate on Scheme lists; list/tree fluency from lab10 is required
